Package io.mdudel.zenoh

Class ZenohClient

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.ZenohClient
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public final class ZenohClient extends Object implements AutoCloseable
Standalone Zenoh native-client publisher wrapper.

Distilled from a larger application's Zenoh emitter and stripped of application-specific hooks (JSON library, framework interfaces, wire format decoders). This is the minimal, dependency-free surface needed to:

  • Open a native Zenoh session against a router endpoint.
  • Configure TLS / mTLS (root CA, client cert + key, hostname verify).
  • Declare a base Publisher on a key expression.
  • Optionally cache per-subkey publishers for base/<sub> routing.
  • Publish byte[] payloads and track basic metrics.

Endpoint syntax

  • tcp/host:port, udp/host:port, ws/host:port — plaintext transports.
  • tls/host:port, quic/host:port, wss/host:port — TLS transports. If a client cert + key are supplied, mTLS is enabled automatically.

Optional org prefix

Fabrics that carve out per-tenant key prefixes (e.g. an ACL-restricted router where each operator publishes under their own org id) can pass a separate org string. It is prepended to the base key expression at publish time as org/keyExpr, with slash normalisation, so re-orging is a one-field edit and unit tests can pin the resolver.

Platform

zenoh-java 1.9.0 bundles native libraries for x86_64-linux-gnu, x86_64-apple-darwin, and x86_64-pc-windows-msvc. On other architectures Zenoh.open(Config) will throw UnsatisfiedLinkError, which is caught as Throwable and re-thrown as IOException with a friendly message.

Thread-safety

start() and stop() are not reentrant — call them once each per instance. publish(byte[]) is safe from multiple threads once started; per-subkey publisher creation is synchronised.

Example


 ZenohClient c = ZenohClient.builder()
         .connectEndpoint("tcp/localhost:7447")
         .keyExpr("demo/example/zenoh-java")
         .build();
 c.start();
 c.publish("hello".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
 c.stop();

      public void publish(byte[] data) throws IOException
      Publish to the base key expression.
      Throws:
      IOException

    • publish

      public void publish(String subKey, byte[] data) throws IOException
      Publish to effectiveKeyExpr/subKey if subKey is non-null and non-empty; otherwise to the base key. Per-sub publishers are declared lazily and cached, so long-running processes should reuse the same subKeys rather than randomising them (avoids unbounded map growth).
      Throws:
      IOException

    • builder

      public static ZenohClient.Builder builder()
